submit_fields.incident_description: I got a $4,200 ER bill for a sprained ankle and my insurance denied it. The hospital itemized statement looks like it charged me for the same ER visit twice. Please review it and find anything I was overcharged for.

claim.incident_description: I got a $4,200 ER bill for a sprained ankle and my insurance denied it. The hospital itemized statement looks like it charged me for the same ER visit twice. Please review it and find anything I was overcharged for.

extracted_data.incident_details: Itemized hospital bill from St. Meridian Regional Medical Center for ER visit on 06/02/2026 for right ankle sprain. Five line items total $4,200 billed; insurance denied claim (Self-Pay) so patient owes full $4,200.
key_findings: J7030 — IV INFUSION, NORMAL SALINE 1000 ML — $450.00 (allowed $0.00)
POSSIBLE ALTERED/SYNTHETIC DOCUMENT: Future service/statement dates in 2026
POSSIBLE ALTERED/SYNTHETIC DOCUMENT: Duplicate identical 99285 charges on same date

fraud_signals.description: CPT 99285 (ER VISIT LEVEL 5) billed twice at $1,800.00 each on the same date for the identical ER visit. Overcharge of $1,800.00 to patient. Fair-price benchmark: single instance of 99285 typically allowed at $350–$550 (Medicare/commercial rates).

CPT 99285 (high-complexity ER visit) used for simple right ankle sprain instead of appropriate lower code such as 99283. One instance overcharge of ~$1,200.00 (after removing duplicate). Fair-price benchmark: 99283 typically allowed at $150–$250.
J7030 (IV normal saline) billed at $450.00. Overcharge of ~$350.00. Fair-price benchmark: $80–$120 allowed amount.

coverage_result.explanation: The damage type 'itemized_bill' is not covered under policy BCBS-PPO-4471-2026 (general). Covered damage types: vehicle collision, fire damage, water damage, theft.
